sanskrit_devanagari:
ये यथा मां प्रपद्यन्ते तांस्तथैव भजाम्यहम् ।
मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥ ११ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
ye yathā māṁ prapadyante tāṁs tathaiva bhajāmy aham |
mama vartmānuvartante manuṣyāḥ pārtha sarvaśaḥ || 11 ||

translations.verbatim_quote: As all surrender unto Me, I reward them accordingly. Everyone follows My path in all respects, O son of Pṛthā.
As all surrender unto Me, I reward them accordingly. Everyone follows My path in all respects, O son of Pritha.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 2748 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: Everyone is searching for Kṛṣṇa in the different aspects of His manifestations. Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, is partially realized in His impersonal brahma-jyotir effulgence and as the all-pervading Supersoul dwelling within everything, including the particles of atoms.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 4.11 Yatha, according to the manner in which, the purpose for which, seeking, whatever fruit; prapadyante, they approach; mam, Me; aham, I; bhajami, favour; tan, them; tatha eva, in that very manner, by granting that fruit. This is the idea. For they are not seekers of Liberation.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-4-11-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara reads the verse as Krishna's claim that he reciprocates each devotee's mode of approach in the same mode — the seeker after liberation receives liberation, the seeker after worldly fruits receives those, etc. The path is the Lord's; the diversity is in the approach.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 4.11 Whoever desirous of resorting to Me, in whatever manner they think of Me according to their inclinations and take refuge in Me, i.e., resort to Me — I favour them in the same manner as desired by them; I reveal Myself to them.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-4-11-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja reads the verse strongly: the Lord, being impartial yet responsive, requites each devotee according to the mode of their approach — meaning the relation is mutual. The verse is not a flat 'all paths are equal' claim; it is a reciprocity claim, where the Lord's reception is shaped by the approach itself.

positions.rendering: As all surrender unto Me, I reward them accordingly
Each is received in the form of their approach (worldly seekers get worldly fruits; liberation-seekers get liberation)
The Lord's reciprocity is real and mode-shaped, not a flat path-equivalence

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: Modern popular readings often flatten this verse to 'all paths are equal.' The classical reading is sharper: the Lord's response IS shaped by the approach. Worldly approach → worldly fruit; liberation-seeking approach → liberation. The diversity is honored, but the differential receptions are also honored.

literal_meaning: As those approach me, so I receive them; humans follow my path in all ways, Pārtha.
traditional_meaning_consensus: Krishna names a reciprocity-principle. Whatever shape one's approach takes, the Lord's reception matches that shape. Sarvaśaḥ — in all ways — humans follow Krishna's path; the path is one but the modes of approach are many. The verse is often quoted as the Gītā's most pluralistic statement.
